title: Remove GS_MAX/GS_MIN static env vars; source everything live from Fantrax
description: Now that GS budget/gscheck fetch real limits live from Fantrax (rosterbot-r10), the GS_MAX/GS_MIN numeric env vars only ever served as a fallback value + feature toggle. Replace with a single GS_TRACKING_ENABLED boolean toggle; on live-fetch failure, send a Pushover alert (PUSHOVER_USER_KEY) instead of falling back to a stale static number. Also remove GS_MAX/GS_MIN from infra/infra.go's ECS secrets and the AWS SSM parameters. Discovered along the way: internal/lineupapi (used by the Lambda in lambda/) transitively imports internal/fantrax, and lambda/go.mod had no replace pin for the go-fantrax fork branch, so cdk synth was already broken on main since PR #29 merged — fixed as part of this work.
notes: PR: https://github.com/nixon-commits/rosterbot/pull/35 (branch worktree-gs-tracking-toggle). Also fixes an unrelated but blocking cdk-deploy failure (lambda/go.mod missing go-fantrax fork pin) that's been broken since PR #29 merged ~9am.
close_reason: GS_TRACKING_ENABLED toggle deployed and verified healthy in production (25 runs, 0 failures since deploy). Fixed unrelated blocking cdk-deploy bug (lambda/go.mod). GS_MAX/GS_MIN SSM parameters deleted, confirmed no remaining references anywhere in the codebase.

title: Fetch live Fantrax GS position limits instead of static GS_MAX/GS_MIN
description: Static GS_MAX/GS_MIN env vars diverge from Fantrax's real per-period limits whenever a scoring period spans more than one calendar week (confirmed: period 1 real 17/21, period 16 real 15/19, both vs configured 10/12). Add go-fantrax GetTeamRosterPositionCounts endpoint, wire internal/fantrax.GetGSLimits, update cmd/optimize.go and internal/gscheck to use it with env-var fallback. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-fantrax-gs-limits-design.md.
close_reason: Live Fantrax GS limits wired into optimize + gscheck with env-var fallback; verified directly against the live league for periods 1/15/16/17 (16=15/19, the All-Star break value). go-fantrax PR: https://github.com/pmurley/go-fantrax/pull/7. Follow-up rosterbot-6ds tracks post-merge go.mod cleanup.

title: Harden optimize.go's period-number resolution to match gscheck's derivation
description:
Final whole-branch review of rosterbot-r10 (live Fantrax GS limits) found that cmd/optimize.go resolves the scoring-period number via GetMatchupWeekNumberForDate (a positional count over matchupWeekRanges' same-opponent date groupings), while internal/gscheck.RunGSCheck uses period.Number (parsed directly from Fantrax's own 'Scoring Period N' caption text). These are structurally independent derivations with no code-level guarantee of agreement -- matchupWeekRanges groups purely by consecutive same-opponent entries with no gap check, so a genuine back-to-back rematch against the same opponent would desync the position count from the real ScoringPeriod number, silently (GetGSLimits would still return valid data for the wrong period, no error/warning).

Verified directly against the live 2026 league (all 23 scoring periods this season, via GetScoringPeriodsAndTeams + GetMatchupWeekNumberForDate cross-check): zero mismatches, including period 16 (the All-Star break this whole effort was built for). So this does NOT manifest as a live bug this season -- filing as a hardening fast-follow, not a regression.

Suggested fix: source the period number in cmd/optimize.go's GS-budget block the same structurally-guaranteed way gscheck.go does -- via GetAllMatchupEntries()'s ScoringPeriod field (or an equivalent lookup keyed off today's date) instead of GetMatchupWeekNumberForDate's positional count. Before landing, verify MatchupEntry.ScoringPeriod is uniform across all entries within a single merged week (e.g. period 16's Jul13-26 span) -- not yet confirmed.

title: Repoint go-fantrax replace at pmurley/go-fantrax once GAMES_PER_POS PR merges
description: rosterbot-r10 temporarily points the go-fantrax replace directive at the nixon-commits/go-fantrax add-games-per-position-endpoint branch (see go.mod). Once https://github.com/pmurley/go-fantrax/pull/7 merges to pmurley/go-fantrax main, run: go mod edit -replace github.com/pmurley/go-fantrax=github.com/nixon-commits/go-fantrax@main (or drop the replace and bump the require version if depending on pmurley/go-fantrax directly is preferred), then go mod tidy, go build ./..., go test ./..., commit.

title: Move run ledger to its own S3 prefix (runledger/) instead of sharing runs/
description:
Follow-up to the /v1/runs pagination fix (PR #27, internal/lineupapi/s3lineup/runs.go). Root cause of that bug: the run ledger (runs/<invts>-<id>.json, listed by RunsStore.recent()) shares the runs/ S3 prefix with per-run output blobs (runs/<hex-id>/output.json, written by OutputStore). Lexicographic key ordering interleaves the two, so listing the ledger requires paginating past an ever-growing pool of unrelated output keys.

PR #27 fixed this on the reader side (recent() now follows NextContinuationToken instead of assuming a single page). That's correct and already deployed, but every /v1/runs and /v1/runs/{id} call still pages through however many output sub-keys happen to sort before the ledger records it needs - cost grows with total run count, not with `limit`.

This ticket is to actually separate the two into distinct prefixes so ledger listing goes back to being a cheap, bounded operation with no dependency on how many output blobs exist.
design:
- Add a new prefix (e.g. runledger/) for ledger records only; leave OutputStore on runs/<id>/output.json (output blobs are looked up by exact key via Get(), never listed, so they don't need to move).
- Update both call sites that construct RunsStore with s3lineup.NewRuns(ctx, bucket, "runs/"):
  - cmd/ledger.go:79 (the run-ledger CLI writer, PutRun)
  - lambda/main.go:42 (the read path, List/Get)
  Change both to "runledger/".
- One-time migration: copy existing runs/<invts>-<id>.json objects (NOT the runs/<hex-id>/output.json sub-objects) to runledger/<invts>-<id>.json in STATE_BUCKET. Can be a small one-off Go script or `aws s3api list-objects-v2` + filtered `aws s3 cp` loop (must exclude keys containing "/" after the runs/ prefix, matching the existing flat-key filter logic in RunsStore.recent()).
- After migration is verified (both old runs/*.json ledger keys and new runledger/*.json exist and /v1/runs still reports correctly against the new prefix), old runs/*.json ledger records can be left in place (harmless, no longer listed) or cleaned up later - not urgent since they're small flat JSON objects.
- Once runledger/ is confirmed to contain only flat ledger keys, recent() no longer strictly needs the "/" sub-key filter or even the pagination loop for correctness (only for scaling past 1000 total ledger records) - but there's no harm in leaving both, they're now just defensive rather than load-bearing.
- Verify old and new list against each other during cutover (e.g. run both prefixes' List side by side for a day) before removing the "runs/" writer path.
acceptance_criteria:
- Ledger writer (cmd/ledger.go) and reader (lambda/main.go) both point at runledger/ prefix
- Existing ledger records migrated from runs/*.json to runledger/*.json with no data loss (spot-check record count and a handful of IDs match)
- GET /v1/runs and /v1/runs/{id} verified live against the new prefix, newest-first, matching pre-migration output
- go test ./... passes with updated s3lineup tests reflecting the new prefix
close_reason: Shipped in #28 (merged aec08ed). Writer (cmd/ledger.go) and reader (lambda/main.go) both on runledger/; infra IAM updated; 409 ledger records migrated from runs/ to runledger/ (verified: independent count match 409=409, byte-identical spot check); GET /v1/runs and /v1/runs/{id} verified live post-deploy, newest-first. Follow-up rosterbot-q3b filed for optional cleanup of the now-orphaned old runs/*.json ledger keys.

title: Delete stale runs/*.json ledger records after runledger/ migration
description: rosterbot-432 migrated 409 run-ledger records from runs/*.json to runledger/*.json (both writer and reader now point at runledger/, verified live). The old runs/*.json ledger objects were deliberately left in place per the migration design (harmless, no longer listed by RunsStore -- only runs/<id>/output.json sub-keys are still read there). This is optional cleanup to reclaim the ~409 stale flat objects once nobody needs to cross-reference the old location; not urgent.
acceptance_criteria: Old flat runs/<invts>-<id>.json ledger keys (as opposed to runs/<id>/output.json sub-keys, which must be preserved) are deleted from the live STATE_BUCKET; runs/<id>/output.json objects and GET /v1/runs are unaffected.
